content:
The Dilemma:
I run a free WebSocket relay service, and my employer is using it to power live(ish) crypto price updates across the site. My service was designed primarily around making short-lived channels, rather than broadcast fanouts (a few price messages in --> loads of price messages out to clients). As such, i built the infra around stable servers... that have to pay for bandwidth egress.
This makes their fanout a potentially dangerous/explosive situation (ofc they've agreed to cover any costs, but that's not the point).
I'm approaching 2 billion messages delivered in the last couple weeks, or around 500GB outbound traffic. Totally fine for now, but as that grows, I need an alternative that's wall-clock-bound rather than egress bandwidth bound.
Proposal:
Cloudflare Durable Objects (IIRC) do not pay for outbound messages at all, have adiscounted fee rate for inbound messages... and mostly just play for wall-clock uptime. On paper this sounds like it would be the perfect solution to always-on broadcast channels. Basically turn that X-->infinity spend into closer to $5 flat. I think each DO can handle like 32K connections, which is more than enough in this particular case.
Question:
Does anyone have experience with CF DO specifically in WebSocket use? Years ago, I'd planned to build the service around that, but the wall-clock model really didn't work for a free/public service, so I had to abandon it and go the dedicated hosted route. Haven't touched that combo since... so I'd love to hear from anyone that's tried it, what they thought, etc. Alternatives are also welcome! Basically just need something that super cheaply/easily handles *only* fanouts - the service as-is crushes the transient usage side of things.

  submitted by   /u/kevin_whitley
[link]   [comments]
